最近，Jezebel收到了一封奇怪的电子邮件，来自一个自称是GetEmails.com联合创始人的人，GetEmails.com是一个全新的出版商受众增长工具。我们被告知，这项技术可以通过向像我们这样的出版物提供在任何一天阅读我们文章的超过三分之一的人的姓名、电子邮件地址和令人难以置信的家庭地址，来识别网站的匿名访问者。
事实上，电子邮件继续说，一些出版商已经利用了这个机会。据推测，这些客户包括塔克·卡尔森(Tucker Carlson)创立的右翼网站“每日来电”(The Daily Caller)，该网站有发表白人民族主义者文章的历史；极端保守的“错误信息工厂”“西部日报”；以及一家专注于股票的行业出版物。
该工具被宣传为出版物留住读者的一种方式，通过向那些可能访问过其网站但忽略了提供联系点的人发送未经请求的时事通讯或订阅优惠。但令我们震惊的是，一家网站可能在访问者不知情的情况下保留了访问者电子邮件和邮寄地址的数据库，而且奇怪的是，选择这样做的出版物似乎大多是右翼分子。初创公司有夸大客户名单的倾向，但通过查看网站的源代码和构建的网站(一个索引给定网站中嵌入内容的网站)，我们证实，我们收到的电子邮件中列出的网站截至7月下旬一直在使用GET电子邮件。
根据Similar Web的数据，“每日来电”没有回复置评请求，上个月有350万独立访问者。如果一个网站多达三分之一的读者被捕获和记录，就像Get Email在其营销材料中声称的那样，那么每个月可能会有超过100万人的姓名、电子邮件地址和邮寄地址，这是一个非常有价值的数据库，其中包含具有非常具体政治观点的人。Get Email的创始人后来告诉我们，呼叫者喜欢他们的服务。他们已经用了几个月了。
无边无际的广告技术已经开发出了各种跟踪用户行为的方法。也就是说，Get Email提供的服务有点超出了行业标准。扎克·爱德华兹(Zach Edwards)，分析公司胜利媒体的创始人，称收到电子邮件的重新身份识别提供了“令人震惊的”和“极其罕见的”。
虽然Get Email表示，它最感兴趣的是将该工具应用于电子商务和营销，但从隐私的角度来看，这种技术确实让人感觉非常具有侵入性。不难想象，一场政治竞选活动有兴趣获得一份所有消费帖子的人的地址清单，在“西部日报”的案例中，这些帖子涉及威胁要接管美国各州的墨西哥政客或投票实施伊斯兰教法的民主党人；从理论上讲，从极端的结论来看，这项技术可以追踪有争议网站的访问者，并收集个人信息，这些信息可以用来欺骗他们或跟踪他们的位置。
“当人们最终明白收发电子邮件是什么时，他们会问的第一个问题是，‘这怎么合法呢？’”
但Get Email背后的人似乎没有意识到这一点，Get Email是两个愚蠢的技术类型，他们在德克萨斯州的奥斯汀和科罗拉多州的阿斯彭之间奔波，认为自己的产品只是一个简单的供需问题。他们告诉我们，包括家庭住址基本上是事后才想到的，他们提供的一小段数据只是因为他们找到了获得这些数据的方法。“当你看到所有的数据，而不是只看到一列电子邮件时，你会有一种恍惚的感觉，你知道吗？”39岁的前雷曼兄弟(Lehman Brothers)员工亚当·罗宾逊(Adam Robinson)说，他和他的女朋友海伦·夏普(Helen Sharp)是公司的公众形象。“我们现在只是生活在这个数据的世界里。这不是我的错。这不是Facebook的错。只是，我们住在里面。“
这对夫妇不太可能是一个具有如此潜在邪恶用途的产品的大使，他们制作了大量奇怪的营销视频，以宣传他们八个月前推出的游客抓取工具，现在他们说每年能带来250万美元的收入。YouTube上的短小广告看起来像是介于行为艺术和二手车停车场广告的业余销售技巧之间的东西。这些视频中有很大一部分，以及Get Email网站上出现的内容，都提到了罗宾逊商业模式的合法性，这种模式在美国被激进地标榜为完全光明正大，如果不是在其他地方的话。
“当人们最终明白什么是GetEmail时，他们会问的第一个问题是，‘这怎么合法？’”这对夫妇在一段视频中说，这段视频似乎是在他们的厨房里拍摄的。在其他帖子中，他们基本上解决了同样的问题，但罗宾逊打扮成汉堡人的样子，象征着他正在“窃取数据”的印象，或者夏普对她男朋友的信息挖掘做法嗤之以鼻，称他为“坏男孩，坏男孩”。在另一段视频中，夏普穿着狐狸服装：“人们喜欢说这是不道德的，因为它是入侵的，就像这种入侵的狐狸！你知道什么才是真正的侵入性吗？脸书！“。这对夫妇以一种呼叫和响应的方式进行中继。
法律问题的简单答案是，没有任何立法接近于解决他们通过将几个可疑的网络实践合并到一个简化的流程中而创建的工具。或者，正如罗宾逊喜欢指出的那样，美国的隐私法和垃圾邮件法是选择退出的，而不是选择加入的。只要你让一个人选择拒绝你寄给他们的东西，你就可以收集和传输一大堆东西。正如罗宾逊告诉我们的那样，他正在使用的数据是“某种程度上的消费者灰色地带”，但对他所服务的公司来说可能是非常有价值的。他说：“我认为这不像是20年的生意。”“这只是现在存在的东西。”
Get Email的起源故事涉及一种在科技行业很常见的偶然产品开发：一项服务在一个单一的上下文中迭代和调整，直到它几乎与其他东西相似。一个面向小企业和电子商务网站的电子邮件营销工具变成了访问者掠夺器，然后成为将网站流量转化为地点和名称列表的一种方式。
罗宾逊是一个和蔼可亲、毫无戒备、头发齐肩的人，我们谈话时他正在阿斯彭。(正如他告诉另一位采访者的那样，几年前，他阅读了每周工作4小时的规定，在大多数日子里，滑雪板从早上9点一直滑到中午。)。他于2003年搬到纽约，在雷曼兄弟(Lehman Brothers)工作，交易信用违约互换(CDS)。但他说，他的室友在他居住的一个地方创办了Vimeo，他“一直想成为一个科技型的人”。因此，在金融危机之后，他创办了一家名为罗布里(Roble)的电子邮件营销公司，到2016年收入将达到500万美元。最近，罗宾逊通过罗布里发现，他提供的一项电子邮件清理服务非常受欢迎，以至于客户都在以非传统的方式使用它。“如果你读到像Y Combinator这样的东西，”他说，“他们谈论的是产品市场契合度是什么感觉，”他指的是你正在建造的东西应该是你的观众最想要的东西。对他来说，这感觉就是那样。因此，他剥离了一家独立的公司，增加了几个数据点，并开始销售他的服务，将网站访问者转换为电子邮件地址、邮政地址和姓名。
总的来说，GetEmail利用了互联网上一些最具欺骗性的服务，同时设法不完全成为一种骗局。在互联网上，各式各样的剥削网站-想想Health surance.com，或者一个可能承诺帮助你找到汽车贷款的网站-将信息输入到各种表格中并将其出售。GetEmail表示，它已经积累了超过3.5亿个从这些公司购买的电子邮件和邮政地址，以及它们所有者的名字和姓氏。
有了这个数据库，GetEmail利用了电子邮件营销服务跟踪互联网用户的一种方式。当你与某些电子邮件时事通讯互动时--比方说，通过点击狗狗兽医主动发送的宣传邮件中嵌入的链接--一个小文件，一个cookie，会存储在你的浏览器内存中，其中可能包含兽医信息发送到的电子邮件地址的加扰版本。根据罗宾逊的说法，GetEmail已经通过与电子邮件营销公司的一系列合作，成功地访问了美国一半的网络流量的这种特定类型的cookie。因此，如果你要访问罗宾逊的一个客户的网站，一段嵌入的代码会将你打乱的电子邮件地址与从Health Thinsurance.com等网站收集的其他信息数据库进行交叉比对，以进行匹配。
罗宾逊说，关于合法性和隐私的自制广告更多的是
罗宾逊似乎在大肆宣扬他利用漏洞的做法和对他的公司所能做的事情感到羞愧之间摇摆不定。当我们的谈话从ROI和市场营销转移到这个工具的更广泛的含义时，我们的谈话就支离破碎了。他一度表示，“每日来电”是他们最知名的客户；后来，他淡化了出版商的角色，并表示，获取电子邮件真的只是小企业给那些带着满满购物车离开网站的人发电子邮件的一种方式。“这就是真正的用例，”他说。
在这对夫妇的一段视频中，罗宾逊反驳了有关他的工具不道德的指控，称利用法律真空是“企业家所做的，嗯，”并点名为Airbnb、苹果和优步。对我们来说，他承认，“如果这就像谷歌一样，他们这样做是为了，就像每个美国人和每个企业都在使用它一样，那就和我们想出如何启动一家初创公司完全不同。”他说，随着公司规模的扩大，可能值得重新审视是否应该将邮政地址与电子邮件和姓名一起刮掉。
几分钟后，罗宾逊告诉我们：“我宁愿公开地说，我认为我们发现了一些东西，我们将以一种我们希望对世界有利的方式遵循经济使用案例。”在随后的一封电子邮件中，他指出，他认为自己的公司是成功的，因为根据他的数据，人们打开的主动电子邮件比他们注册的电子邮件更多。他在接受我们采访时说：“这是一件非常新的事情。”“企业家精神并不完全是非黑即白的东西。”
营销专业人员创造的监控工具的含义与专业人员本身之间的反差-乐观、高度专注，被自己之外的“用例”弄得迷惑不解-通常会造成这样一次认知上不和谐的采访。我们交谈几天后，罗宾逊和夏普上传了一段新视频，视频中这对夫妇扮成唐纳德和伊万卡·特朗普(Donald And Ivanka Trump)，宣传“免费电子邮件大赠送”(Great Get Email Giveaway)，这是“支付支票保护法案”(Paycheck Protection Act)的恶作剧“替代品”。夏普用模拟的东欧口音说，所有这些数据的公布可能会在未来10年为企业提供“超过1000万美元的刺激”。
note_en:
Recently, Jezebel received a curious email from someone who identified themselves as a co-founder of GetEmails.com, “an all new audience growth tool for publishers.” The technology, we were told, could identify anonymous visitors to a website by providing publications like us with the names, email address, and, incredibly, home addresses of over a third of the people who were reading our stuff on any given day.
  In fact, the email continued, some publishers had already taken advantage of this opportunity. These clients supposedly included  The Daily Caller, the right-wing website founded by Tucker Carlson with a history of publishing articles written by white nationalists; the hyper-conservative “  misinformation mill”  Western Journal; and a trade publication focusing on stocks.
 The tool was promoted as a way for publications to retain readers by sending unsolicited newsletters or subscription offers to people who may have visited their sites but neglected to provide a point of contact. But it struck us as ominous that a website might keep a database of visitors’ emails and postal addresses, presumably without their knowledge, and odd that the publications opting to do so would appear to be mostly right-wing. Startups have a tendency to exaggerate their client list, but by taking a look at the websites’ source code and Built With, a website that indexes what’s embedded in a given website, we confirmed that the sites listed in the email we received had been using Get Emails as of late July.
 The Daily Caller, which didn’t return a request for comment, had 3.5 million unique visitors last month, according to Similar Web. If up to a third of a website’s readership was being captured and logged, as Get Emails claims in its marketing material, that could be over a million names, email addresses, and postal addresses every month, an incredibly valuable database of people with very specific political views.  The Caller, Get Emails’ founders later told us, love their service. They’ve been using it for a few months.
 The sprawling universe of advertising technology has developed all kinds of ways to track user behavior. That said, what Get Emails is offering goes somewhat beyond industry standards. Zach Edwards, the founder of the analytics firm Victory Medium, called the type of re-identification that Get Emails offers “outrageous” and “extremely uncommon.”
   While Get Emails says it’s most interested in applying the tool for e-commerce and marketing, from a privacy standpoint, this kind of technology  does feel wildly invasive. It isn’t hard to imagine a political campaign being interested in obtaining a list of all the addresses of people who consume posts about, in the Western Journals’ case,   Mexican politicians threatening to take over U.S. states or   Democrats voting to impose Sharia Law; theoretically, taken to an extreme conclusion, this technology could track visitors to controversial websites and glean personal information that could be used to doxx them or keep tabs on where they are.
 “When someone finally understands what Get Emails is the first question they ask is, ‘How is this legal?”
 But this hasn’t appeared to have occurred to the people behind Get Emails, a pair of goofy tech types who split time between Austin, Texas, and Aspen, Colorado and consider their product a simple issue of supply and demand. The inclusion of home addresses, they tell us, was mostly an afterthought, a snippet of data they offered simply because they found a way to get it. “There’s an a-ha moment when you see all that data versus just seeing a column of emails, you know?” says Adam Robinson, a 39-year-old former Lehman Brothers employee who, along with his girlfriend Helen Sharp, is the public face of the company. “And we just live in this world of data now. It’s not my fault. It’s not Facebook’s fault. It’s just that, we live in it.”
  The couple are unlikely ambassadors for a product with such potentially nefarious uses, and they have created a   prolific output of bizarre marketing videos to promote the visitor-scraping tool they launched eight months ago and now say is bringing in $2.5 million a year. The short Youtube spots come off as something between performance art and the amateur salesmanship of an ad for a used car lot. A huge chunk of those videos, as well as what appears on Get Emails’ website, address the legitimacy of Robinsons’ business model, which is aggressively billed as completely above-board in the United States, if not really anywhere else.
 “When someone finally understands what GetEmails is the first question they ask is, ‘How is this legal?” the couple says in one video that appears to have been shot in their kitchen. In other posts, they address essentially the same issue, but with Robinson   dressed as the Hamburgler to symbolize the perception he’s “stealing data” or Sharp pouting at her boyfriend’s information-mining practices and calling him a “  bad, bad boy.” In another video, Sharp wears a fox costume: “People love telling it’s unethical because it’s invasive, like this invasive species of fox! You know what’s really invasive? Facebook!” the couple relays in a sort of call-and-response.
   The simple answer to the legal question is that there isn’t legislation that comes close to addressing the tool they’ve created by merging several shady web practices into a single streamlined process. Or, as Robinson likes to point out, privacy and spam laws in the United States are opt-out, not opt-in. As long as you give a person the option to reject what you’re sending them, you can collect and transmit a whole trove of stuff. As Robinson told us, the data he’s using is “kind of a consumer gray area,” but can be incredibly valuable to the companies he serves. “This is not something that I think is like a 20-year business to be in,” he says. “It’s just something that exists now.’
  The origin story of Get Emails involves the kind of accidental product development that’s common in the tech sector: A service is iterated upon and tweaked within a single context until it almost resembles something else. An email marketing tool for small businesses and e-commerce sites becomes a visitor scraper and then a way to turn website traffic into a list of locations and names.
 Robinson, an affable and unguarded guy with hair to his shoulders, was in Aspen when we spoke. (  As he told another interviewer, he read the four-hour work week several years ago and, on most days, skis from 9AM to noon.) He moved to New York in 2003 and worked for Lehman Brothers, trading credit default swaps. But his roommate, he says, started Vimeo in one of the places he lived in and he “always wanted to be this tech guy.” So after the financial crisis, he started an email-marketing business called Robly that was making   $5 million in revenue by 2016. Recently, through Robly, Robinson found that an email-scraping service he offered was so popular his customers were using it in unconventional ways. “If you read stuff like Y Combinator,” he says, “they talk about what product market fit feels like,” referring to the idea that the thing you’re building should be the thing your audience is clamoring for the most.  This felt, to him, like that. So he spun off a separate company, added a few more data points, and started selling his service that translates a website visitor into an email address, a postal address, and a name.
 Broadly, GetEmails exploits some of the most deceitful services  on the internet while managing to not quite become a scam itself. Across the internet, networks of exploitative websites—think healthinsurance.com, or a site that might promise to help you find an auto loan—save information entered into their various forms and put it up for sale. GetEmails says it has amassed more than 350 million email and postal addresses buying from these companies, as well as the first and last name of their owners.
 Armed with this database, GetEmails takes advantage of one of the ways that email marketing services track users across the internet. When you interact with certain email newsletters—say, by clicking a link that’s embedded in a unsolicited promotional email from your dog’s vet—a small file, a cookie, is stored in your browser’s memory, which may contain a scrambled version of the email address to where the vet’s message was sent. According to Robinson, GetEmails has managed to access this specific kind of cookie for half of all web traffic in the United States through a series of partnerships with email marketing companies. So if you were to visit the website of one of Robinson’s customers, a piece of embedded code would cross-reference your scrambled email address with the database of other information gleaned from sites like healthinsurance.com to make a match.
   Robinson says the home-made ads about legality and privacy were more or less a ploy to go viral. He says his first explainer video about his service brought in a new audience, if one that was vehemently opposed to his practices. “It was basically like, you guys are scumbags, you shouldn’t do this, this anti-privacy type of comment we were getting.” But, he says, the app also started to do “unbelievably well.” He and Sharp decided to keep making controversial ads, he says, “knowing that the people are haters and would never buy it anyway are going to help us get distribution from it.” When Jezebel asked if Robinson was trolling, he balked. “It’s all very experimental,” he says. “I don’t want it to be like Donald Trump.”
 Robinson appears to oscillate between trumpeting his exploitation of loopholes and feeling sheepish about what his company can do. Our conversation sputtered when it moved away from ROIs and marketing and into the broader implications of the tool. At one point, he said the Daily Caller was their most well-known client; later, he downplayed the role of publishers and said Get Emails was really just a way for small businesses to email people who’d left a website with a full shopping cart. “That’s really the use case,” he said.
 In one of the couple’s videos, Robinson countered charges that his tool was unethical by saying that exploiting legal vacuums is “  what entrepreneurs do, duh,” and named AirBnB, Apple, and Uber. To us, he admitted that “if this were like Google and they were doing it for, like, every single American and every single business were using it, that is a totally different deal than us figuring out how to get a startup off the ground.” As the company got larger, he said, it might be worth reviewing whether postal addresses should be scraped along with emails and names.
 A few minutes later, Robinson told us, “I would rather be on the record saying, I think that we discovered something and we will follow the economic use cases in a way that we hope is good for the world.” In a follow-up email, he noted that he considered his company successful because according to his data people opened up unsolicited emails more than ones they’d signed up for. “This thing is very new,” he said in our interview. “And entrepreneurism is not something that’s totally black and white.”
 The contrast between the implications of surveillance tools created by marketing professionals and the professionals themselves—optimistic, hyper-focused, flummoxed by “use cases” outside of their own—usually makes for a cognitively dissonant interview like this. A few days after we spoke, Robinson and Sharp uploaded a new video in which the couple dressed up as Donald and Ivanka Trump to promote the “Great Get Emails Giveaway,” a hokey “replacement” for the Paycheck Protection Act. The release of all this data, Sharp said in a mock Eastern European accent, could provide businesses with “over 10 million dollars in stimulus” over the next 10 years.

note:
不仅仅是床位和医疗用品-多个州的医院都在减少医生和护士，以应对新冠肺炎患者的洪流。
据“洛杉矶时报”报道，军方医务人员周五抵达洛杉矶县，增援两家在疫情中苦苦挣扎的地区医院的工作人员。在州官员通过联邦紧急事务管理署提出请求后，国防部部署了空军医疗队。
泰晤士报指出，该州其他六家医院已经在抗击这种新型冠状病毒的斗争中得到了军事支持。
州长紧急服务办公室发言人布莱恩·弗格森(Brian Ferguson)告诉“泰晤士报”，增加的医务人员“基本上是为了向有需要的医院提供激增的人员。”他指出，苦苦挣扎的医院正面临病人激增，也可能人手不足，可能是因为工作人员本身也感染了大流行性冠状病毒。
此外，接收军事人员的两家洛杉矶县医院在一份声明中写道，他们将使用这些人员“在新冠肺炎住院人数不断增加的情况下支持医院重症监护病房的运作”。
其他几个州可能很快也会遇到类似的情况。根据乔治华盛顿大学(George Washington University)研究人员最近的一份报告，亚利桑那州和德克萨斯州下个月预计将面临重症监护病房医生短缺的问题。研究人员估计，仅在8月份，就需要100%以上的州重症监护医生来照顾新冠肺炎病人。
研究人员还报告说，其他11个州也面临人员配备压力。这意味着在接下来的几个月里，该州超过50%的重症监护医生可能需要为新冠肺炎患者提供支持。
研究人员在报告中写道：“新闻媒体在很大程度上关注住院和ICU床位供应耗尽的危险，但这些床位的人员配备可能是一个更大的问题。”“可以在其他医院单位，甚至在医院设置之外设立新床位，但要找到高度专业化的ICU专业人员需要时间。”
报告还指出，大流行还将给现有工作人员带来压力，他们将不得不更多、更努力地轮班工作，以照顾涌入的患者。这反过来可能会导致更多的职业倦怠和更多的医护人员感染。
到目前为止，美国已经报告了430多万例新冠肺炎病例，近15万人死亡。根据纽约时报的追踪，目前30个州的新病例正在增加。
note_en:
It’s not just beds and medical supplies—hospitals in multiple states are running low on doctors and nurses to tend to the deluge of COVID-19 patients.
 Military medical personnel arrived in Los Angeles County Friday to reinforce staffing at two area hospitals struggling amid the pandemic,  according to a report in the Los Angeles Times. The Department of Defense deployed the Air Force medical teams after state officials put in a request through the Federal Emergency Management Agency.
 The Times noted that six other hospitals in the state have already received military back-up in their fight against the novel coronavirus.
 Brian Ferguson, a spokesperson for the Governor’s Office of Emergency Services, told the Times that the additional medical personnel is “basically to provide surge staffing to hospitals in need.” He noted that the struggling hospitals are facing surges in patients and also may be short-staffed, potentially because staff have itself become infected with the pandemic coronavirus.
 Further, the two Los Angeles County hospitals receiving military staff wrote in a statement that they will use the personnel “to support operation of hospital critical care units amidst growing COVID-19 hospitalizations.”
 Several other states may soon be in similar situations. Arizona and Texas are projected to face shortages of doctors who work in intensive care units next month,  according to a recent report by researchers at George Washington University. Researchers estimate that more than 100 percent of the states’ intensive care doctors will be needed to care for COVID-19 patients alone in August.
 Eleven other states are facing staffing strain, the researchers also reported. That means more than 50 percent of the states’ intensive care doctors may be required to support COVID-19 patients in the coming months.
 “The news media [have] largely focused on hospitalizations and the danger of depleting the ICU bed supply, but staffing these beds may be an even greater problem,” the researchers write in the report. “New beds can be set up in other hospital units, or even outside the hospital setting, but it takes time to find highly specialized ICU professionals.”
 The report also noted that the pandemic will also stress current staff, who will have to work more and harder shifts to care for the influx in patients. That in turn could lead to more burnout and more infections among health care workers.
 So far, the United States has reported more than 4.3 million COVID-19 cases and nearly 150,000 deaths. New cases are currently increasing in 30 states, according to  tracking by The New York Times.
title: 随着新冠肺炎的涌入，各州的医生和护士都快用完了
title_en: States are running out of doctors and nurses as COVID-19 surges

民权活动家和许多民主党人严厉批评Facebook拒绝事实核查或删除政治候选人的欺骗性帖子或广告的政策，他们说这一立场有利于唐纳德·特朗普总统的连任竞选活动，忽视了美国长期以来压制选民的努力。他们还批评Facebook在今年早些时候留下了一个帖子，当时特朗普似乎威胁说，参加社会正义抗议的人可能会被枪杀。
扎克伯格的最新评论也是在Facebook上疯传的一段视频之后发布的，视频中关于这种新型冠状病毒的说法是不正确的，也是危险的。在Facebook将其撤下之前，它已经获得了数百万的点击量。
另一方面，虚假信息并不是周三听证会声明的目的。反垄断小组委员会主席、众议员大卫·西西林(D-RI.)召集了这个小组，讨论随着该行业的整合，科技巨头之间的竞争。
扎克伯格的书面评论包括含蓄地抨击民主党人对科技巨头崛起的担忧。
扎克伯格的话说：“据我所知，我们的法律并不仅仅是因为公司规模大而不好。”这句话呼应了该委员会共和党人早些时候的评论。“许多无法竞争的大公司不复存在。这就是为什么我们专注于建造和更新我们的产品，为人们提供尽可能好的体验。“。
扎克伯格的开场白还提到了中国科技公司的崛起如何塑造了这个行业。
“虽然全世界的人都在使用我们的产品，但Facebook是一家引以为豪的美国公司，”评论说。“我们相信美国经济赖以生存的价值观--民主、竞争、包容和自由表达。许多其他科技公司分享这些价值观，但不能保证我们的价值观会胜出。
例如，中国正在建设自己版本的互联网，专注于截然不同的想法，他们正在向其他国家输出他们的愿景，他补充道。当国会和其他利益相关者考虑反垄断法如何支持美国的竞争时，我认为重要的是维护开放和公平的核心价值观，这些价值观使美国的数字经济成为这里和世界各地的一股赋权和机会的力量。“
note_en:
“We recognize that we have a responsibility to stop bad actors from interfering with or undermining these conversations through misinformation, attempted voter suppression, or speech that is hateful or incites violence,” Zuckerberg’s statement says. “I understand the concerns people have in these areas, and we are working to address them. While we are making progress — for example, we have dramatically improved our ability to proactively find and remove harmful content and prevent election interference — I recognize that we have more to do.”
  Civil rights activists and many Democrats have castigated Facebook for its policy of refusing to fact-check or remove deceptive posts or ads from political candidates, a stance they say benefits President Donald Trump's reelection campaign and ignores a long history of voter suppression efforts in the U.S. They have also criticized Facebook for leaving up a post earlier this year in which Trump appeared to threaten that people taking part in social justice protests might be shot.
 Zuckerberg's newest comment also comes in the wake of news about a video going viral on Facebook that featured incorrect and dangerous claims about the novel coronavirus. It racked up millions of views before Facebook took it down.
 On the other hand, disinformation is not the stated purpose of Wednesday's hearing. Rep. David Cicilline (D-R.I.), the chairman of the antitrust subcommittee, convened the panel to talk about competition among tech giants as the industry has consolidated.
  Zuckerberg’s written remarks include an implicit jab at Democrats’ concerns about the emergence of tech behemoths.
 “As I understand our laws, companies aren’t bad just because they are big,” Zuckerberg's remarks say, echoing an earlier comment from the panel's Republicans. “Many large companies that fail to compete cease to exist. This is why we’re focused on building and updating our products to give people the best possible experiences.”
 Zuckerberg’s opening remarks also make one reference to how the ascent of Chinese tech companies has shaped the industry.
 “Although people around the world use our products, Facebook is a proudly American company,” the remarks say. “We believe in values — democracy, competition, inclusion and free expression — that the American economy was built on. Many other tech companies share these values, but there’s no guarantee our values will win out.
 "For example, China is building its own version of the internet focused on very different ideas, and they are exporting their vision to other countries," he added. "As Congress and other stakeholders consider how antitrust laws support competition in the U.S., I believe it’s important to maintain the core values of openness and fairness that have made America’s digital economy a force for empowerment and opportunity here and around the world.”

note:
1982年7月，当我正在编写Macintosh工具箱的Control Manager部分时，我的朋友比尔·巴奇(Bill Budge)邀请我参加一个名为“创造性思维”(Creative Think)的计算机行业研讨会，有趣的人发表了据称鼓舞人心的演讲。它是由罗杰·范·奥克(Roger Van Oech)组织的，他是一名顾问，曾写过一本关于创造力的书，书名是“头部一侧的一记重击”。我通常避开创意研讨会和行业闲聊活动，但我的朋友比尔不知何故弄到了免费门票，我想仅仅是去看他一眼就值得了。
卡弗·米德(Carver Mead)就新兴的超大规模集成电路(VLSI)技术做了一个有趣的演讲，一些老芯片业老手讲述了有趣的战争故事，但对我来说，当天的最后一次演讲才是最重要的。它是由艾伦·凯(Alan Kay)提出的，他是Smalltalk和Alto的发明者，也是施乐公司(Xerox Parc)背后的推动力。我读过关于艾伦的报道，并受到他在1977年9月号“科学美国人”上的文章的启发，但我以前从未见过他本人，也从未听说过太多关于他的事情。艾伦的演讲很有启发性，也许是我听过的最鼓舞人心的演讲。当他一个接一个地说了一句又一句精辟的话时，我变得越来越兴奋，并拿出笔记本尽可能多地写下来。艾伦清楚地阐述了我所做工作背后的价值，尽管他并没有意识到这一点，这种方式真的引起了我的共鸣。回到库比蒂诺的办公室后，我把它转录到一页纸上，然后复印下来分发给团队的其他成员。我仍然保留着那些笔记，所以我想在这里复制它们会很有趣，作为激励我们努力的一些想法的一个例子。艾伦·凯1982年7月20日在创造性思维研讨会上的演讲演讲提纲：隐喻、磁场、势利和口号预测未来的最好方法就是发明未来。人类喜欢幻想和分享：幻想满足了对一个更简单、更可控世界的需求。分享很重要--我们都是通信瘾君子。我们有令人难以置信的带宽差距(很容易吸收，很难发放)；我们的设备有互惠的差距(很难吸收，很容易发放)的比喻：电脑是媒介(就像粘土或颜料)，电脑是交通工具，电脑是乐器磁场：找到一个中心隐喻，它是如此美好，以至于一切都与之一致。设计会议不再是必要的，它自己设计。比喻应该干脆有趣。Smalltalk是面向对象的，但它应该是面向消息的。势利：对好主意嗤之以鼻。你必须致力于伟大的想法，而不是好的想法。欣赏平凡：毕竟，铅笔是高科技的一个目标：计算机消失在环境中，计算机不应该表现得好像它无所不知。编程语言的整个概念都是错误的。口号：更好的是最好的相对判断的敌人在艺术中没有立足之地系统程序员是低级崇拜的高级祭司观点值80智商好点子不要经常标尺记住，这都是软件，它只是取决于你什么时候把它具体化。真正看重软件的人应该自己做硬件。最后一条建议：内容重于形式，尽情享受吧。
note_en:
In July of 1982, while I was in the midst of writing the Control Manager part of the Macintosh toolbox, my friend Bill Budge invited me to a computer industry seminar called "Creative Think", where interesting people gave purportedly inspiring talks. It was organized by Roger van Oech, a consultant who had written a book about creativity entitled "A Whack On The Side Of The Head". I usually avoided both creativity seminars and industry schmooze-fests, but my friend Bill had somehow finagled free tickets and I thought it would be worth it just to see him.
 Carver Mead gave an interesting talk on newly emerging VLSI technology, and some old chip industry veterans recounted amusing war stories, but the last talk of the day was the one that mattered to me. It was given by Alan Kay, the inventor of Smalltalk and the Alto, and the driving force behind Xerox PARC. I had read about Alan, and been inspired by his article in the September 1977 issue of Scientific American, but I had never seen him before in person or heard much about him.     Alan's speech was revelatory and was perhaps the most inspiring talk that I ever attended. I grew increasingly excited as he made one brilliant, insightful remark after another, and took out my notebook to write as much of it down as I could. Alan was articulating the values behind the work that I was doing, even though he wasn't aware of it, in a way that really resonated with me. After I got back to my office in Cupertino, I transcribed it onto a single page, and copied it to give out to the rest of team.  I still have those notes, so I thought it would be interesting to reproduce them here, as an example of some of the thinking that inspired our efforts.     Alan Kay's talk at Creative Think seminar, July 20, 1982  Outline of talk: Metaphors, Magnetic Fields, Snobbery and Slogans  The best way to predict the future is to invent it.  Humans like fantasy and sharing:  Fantasy fulfills a need for a simpler, more controllable world.   Sharing is important - we're all communication junkies. We have an incredible bandwidth disparity (easy to take in, hard to give out); our devices have the reciprocal disparity (hard to take in, easy to give out)  Metaphors:  Computer as medium (like clay or paint)  Computer as vehicle  Computer as musical instrument  Magnetic Fields:  Find a central metaphor that's so good that everything aligns to it. Design meetings are no longer necessary, it designs itself. The metaphor should be crisp and fun.  Smalltalk is object-oriented, but it should have been message oriented.  Snobbery:  Turn up your nose at good ideas. You must work on great ideas, not good ones.  Appreciate mundanity: after all, a pencil is high technology  One goal: the computer disappears into the environment  The computer shouldn't act like it knows everything.  The whole notion of 'programming language' is wrong.  Slogans:  Better is the enemy of best  Relative judgements have no place in art  Systems programmers are high priests of a low cult  Point of view is worth 80 IQ points  Good ideas don't often scale  Remember, it's all software, it just depends on when you crystallize it.  People who are really serious about software should make their own hardware.  Final advice: content over form, go for fun.
title: 安迪·赫茨菲尔德(Andy Hertzfeld)1982年7月艾伦·凯(Alan Kay)演讲的笔记
title_en: Notes by Andy Hertzfeld on a Talk by Alan Kay from July 1982

受Ryzen™和EPYC™处理器销售的推动，收入同比增长26%。(环球通讯社)--AMD(纳斯达克市场代码：AMD)今天宣布，2020年第二季度收入为19.3亿美元，营业收入为1.73亿美元，净收入为1.57亿美元，稀释后每股收益为0.13美元。在非GAAP*基础上，营业收入为2.33亿美元，净收入为2.16亿美元，稀释后每股收益为0.18美元。
AMD总裁兼首席执行官丽莎·苏博士说：“我们第二季度业绩强劲，这主要得益于创纪录的笔记本电脑和服务器处理器销售，因为Ryzen和EPYC的收入比一年前翻了一番以上。尽管宏观经济存在一些不确定性，但随着我们在多个市场的业务加速推动我们进入下一个增长阶段，我们正在提高全年收入预期。”
收入为19.3亿美元，同比增长26%，主要是由于计算和图形部门收入增加。收入环比增长8%，主要是由于企业、嵌入式和半定制部门收入增加。
毛利率为44%，同比上升3个百分点，环比下降2个百分点。与去年同期相比的增长主要是由瑞森™和EPYC™处理器的销售推动的。季度环比下降是由于半定制产品销售增加。
营业收入为1.73亿美元，而一年前的营业收入为5900万美元，上一季度为1.77亿美元。非GAAP营业收入为2.33亿美元，而一年前的营业收入为1.11亿美元，上一季度为2.36亿美元。营业收入同比增长，主要是由于收入增长以及Ryzen和EPYC处理器销售的更大比例推动的。
净收益为1.57亿美元，而一年前的净收益为3500万美元，上一季度为1.62亿美元。非GAAP净收益为2.16亿美元，而一年前的净收益为9200万美元，上一季度为2.22亿美元。
稀释后每股收益为0.13美元，而一年前的稀释后每股收益为0.03美元，上一季度为0.14美元。非GAAP稀释后每股收益为0.18美元，而一年前的稀释后每股收益为0.08美元，上一季度为0.18美元。
计算和图形部门的收入为13.7亿美元，同比增长45%，环比下降5%。在Ryzen处理器销售强劲的推动下，收入同比增长。季度环比下降是由于图形处理器销售下降。在Ryzen处理器销售的推动下，客户处理器平均售价(ASP)同比上涨。由于Ryzen移动处理器销售额的较高百分比，客户端处理器ASP季度环比下降。
营业收入为2亿美元，而一年前为2200万美元，上一季度为2.62亿美元。同比增长是由更高的收入推动的。季度环比下降是由于运营费用上升和收入下降。
企业、嵌入式和半定制部门的收入为5.65亿美元，同比下降4%，季度环比增长62%。收入同比下降，原因是半定制产品销售额下降，很大程度上被EPYC处理器销售额上升所抵消。季度环比增长是由于EPYC处理器和半定制产品销售额的增加，其营业收入为3300万美元，而去年同期为8900万美元，上一季度的营业亏损为2600万美元。同比下降是由于运营费用上升和收入下降。季度环比增长是由更高的收入推动的。
所有其他运营亏损为6000万美元，而一年前的运营亏损为5200万美元，上一季度为5900万美元。
凭借独特的云计算、企业计算和高性能计算(HPC)胜利，数据中心采用第二代AMD EPYC处理器的速度继续加快。谷歌宣布推出新的机密虚拟机，用于谷歌计算引擎，独家采用AMD EPYC处理器，采用AMD Secure Encrypted Virtualization。这些保密虚拟机通过AMD EPYC处理器上的高级安全功能为客户提供数据安全和高性能云计算。
Amazon Web Services(AWS)为运行计算密集型工作负载的客户添加了第六个基于AMD EPYC处理器的云实例系列，即新的Amazon EC2 C5a实例。
甲骨文和AMD宣布，AMD EPYC处理器将为新的Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Compute E3平台带来业界领先的内存带宽和核心计数，用于通用和高带宽工作负载。
戴尔技术、HPE、IBM Cloud、Nutanix、SuperMicro、VMware和其他公司宣布推出采用全新AMD EPYC 7Fx2处理器系列的产品，可为企业工作负载带来高频处理能力和每核性能领先优势。
戴尔技术发布了其首款基于AMD EPYC处理器的超融合基础架构系统VxRail E系列。
新的NVIDIA DGX A100系统利用AMD EPYC处理器的性能、内核数量和PCIe®4支持来加速各种人工智能工作负载，如数据分析、培训和推理。
在Ryzen PRO性能、安全功能和针对最苛刻的业务环境的无缝管理的推动下，AMD客户端处理器的商业势头正在增长。联想和AMD宣布推出联想ThinkStationP620，搭载AMD Ryzen Threadriper™PRO处理器，包括64核专业工作站cpu。新的AMD Ryzen ThreadRipper PRO处理器系列具有领先的性能、无与伦比的带宽和AMD PRO技术(包括企业级安全性和可管理性功能)。
AMD宣布推出AMD Ryzen PRO 4000系列移动处理器，为现代IT基础设施带来终极性能、随时随地工作的灵活性、安全功能和无缝可管理性。由AMD Ryzen PRO 4000系列驱动的企业级笔记本电脑现可从惠普和联想购买。
AMD通过第三代AMD Ryzen 3000XT系列台式机处理器扩展了其台式机处理器产品组合，具有更高的提升频率，与上一代相比，在游戏和内容创建方面提供了卓越的性能。
苹果发布了最新的16英寸MacBook Pro，采用了AMD Radeon™Pro 5600M移动图形处理器，为移动用户带来了台式机级的图形性能。
AMD宣布推出AMD RADEON™Pro VII工作站显卡，采用AMD Infinity Fabric™Link技术，为广播和工程专业人员提供卓越的图形和计算性能。
AMD宣布，作为AMD高性能计算基金新冠肺炎研究的一部分，它将贡献基于云的超级计算系统，该系统采用AMD EPYC处理器和AMD Radeon本能加速器。这些系统将与企鹅计算公司合作，交付给纽约大学(NYU)、麻省理工学院(MIT)和莱斯大学，为世界各地选定的研究人员提供远程超级计算能力。
AMD的展望声明基于当前的预期，并考虑了当前的新冠肺炎环境、全球经济背景和客户需求信号。以下陈述为前瞻性陈述，实际结果可能因市场状况、新冠肺炎因素以及下文“警示声明”中陈述的因素而存在实质性差异。
AMD预计2020年第三季度的收入约为25.5亿美元，正负1亿美元，同比增长约42%，环比增长32%，预计同比和环比增长将主要受到Ryzen和EPYC处理器销售以及下一代半定制产品的推动。AMD预计2020年第三季度非GAAP毛利率约为44%。毛利率预计将同比增长，主要受Ryzen和EPYC处理器销售的推动。
AMD现在预计，在PC、游戏和数据中心产品强劲的推动下，2020年的收入将比2019年增长约32%。非GAAP毛利率预计约为45%。
AMD将于下午2点为金融界召开电话会议。PT(下午5：00)。ET)今天讨论其2020年第二季度的财务业绩。AMD将在其网站www.amd.com的投资者关系页面上提供电话会议的实时音频广播。电话会议结束后，网络直播将持续12个月。
在截至2020年6月27日和2020年3月28日的三个月里，GAAP稀释每股收益计算包括与公司2026年可转换票据相关的3100万股股票，以及相关的300万美元和400万美元的利息支出，如果转换为这种方法，则会重新增加到净收入中。
在截至2019年6月29日的三个月里，与公司2026年可转换票据相关的1.06亿股股票没有包括在GAAP稀释每股收益计算中，因为它们的纳入将是反稀释的。
在截至2020年6月27日、2020年3月28日和2019年6月29日的三个月里，非GAAP稀释每股收益计算分别包括与公司2026年可转换票据相关的3100万股、3100万股和1.06亿股，以及相关的100万美元、200万美元和500万美元的利息支出，这些都是根据转换方法计算的净收入。
50年来，AMD一直推动高性能计算、图形和可视化技术的创新，这些技术是游戏、沉浸式平台和数据中心的构建块。世界各地数以亿计的消费者、财富500强领先企业和尖端科研机构每天都依赖AMD技术来改善他们的生活、工作和娱乐方式。AMD在世界各地的员工都专注于制造伟大的产品，以突破可能的界限。有关AMD如何实现今天和鼓舞人心的明天的更多信息，请访问AMD(纳斯达克股票代码：AMD)网站、博客、Facebook和Twitter页面。
本新闻稿包含有关Advanced Micro Devices公司的前瞻性陈述。例如，AMD对下一阶段增长的预期；AMD产品的特点、功能、性能、可用性、时机和预期效益；以及AMD预期的2020年第三季度前景，包括收入和非公认会计准则毛利率以及预期驱动因素，以及基于当前预期和预期的当前新冠肺炎环境、全球经济背景和客户需求信号(根据私人证券诉讼改革的安全港条款做出的)，以及AMD 2020财年的预期财务展望，包括收入和非公认会计准则毛利率和预期驱动因素。前瞻性陈述通常由诸如&#34；将、&#34；&34；可能、&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34；&#34。请投资者注意，本新闻稿中的前瞻性陈述是基于当前的信念、假设和预期，仅在本新闻稿发布之日发表，涉及可能导致实际结果与当前预期大不相同的风险和不确定因素。这类陈述会受到某些已知和未知的风险和不确定性的影响，其中许多风险和不确定性很难预测，通常超出AMD的控制范围，这些风险和不确定性可能会导致实际结果和其他未来事件与前瞻性信息和陈述中表达的、暗示的或预测的情况大不相同。可能导致实际结果与当前预期大不相同的重要因素包括但不限于：英特尔公司在微处理器市场的主导地位及其积极的商业实践；第三方制造商及时生产足够数量的AMD产品并使用有竞争力的技术的能力；AMD产品的预期制造产量；AMD及时推出具有为客户提供价值的功能和性能水平的产品的能力；AMD产生足够的收入和运营现金流或获得ExEx的能力。
在这份收益新闻稿中，除了GAAP财务业绩，AMD还提供了非GAAP财务指标，包括非GAAP毛利率、非GAAP运营费用、非GAAP运营收入、非GAAP净收入和非GAAP每股收益。这些非GAAP财务指标反映了本收益新闻稿表格中所示的某些调整。AMD在计算非GAAP所得税拨备时使用归一化税率，以在报告期间提供更好的一致性。在2020会计年度，AMD使用的预计非GAAP税率约为3%，其中不包括税前非GAAP调整的直接税收影响，这反映了目前可用的信息。AMD还提供了调整后的EBITDA和自由现金流量，作为对其业绩的补充非GAAP衡量标准。这些项目在本收益新闻稿末尾提供的选定公司数据表的脚注中进行了定义。AMD之所以提供这些财务衡量标准，是因为它认为这种非GAAP陈述使投资者更容易比较其本期和历史时期的经营业绩，还因为AMD认为它通过排除它认为不能表明其核心经营业绩的项目以及选定数据表的脚注中描述的其他原因，帮助投资者在一致的基础上比较AMD在各个报告期的表现。“。请参阅本收益新闻稿末尾的数据表。
AMD、AMD箭头标识、EPYC、RADEON、Ryzen、Athlon及其组合是Advanced Micro Devices公司的商标。其他名称仅供参考，用于标识公司和产品，可能是其所有者的商标。
计算和图形部门主要包括台式机和笔记本处理器和芯片组、离散和集成图形处理单元(GPU)、数据中心和专业GPU，以及开发服务。该公司还许可其知识产权组合的一部分。
企业、嵌入式和半定制部分主要包括服务器和嵌入式处理器、半定制的片上系统(SoC)产品、游戏控制台的开发服务和技术。该公司还许可其知识产权组合的一部分。
所有其他类别主要包括未分配给任何运营部门的某些费用和信用。这一类别还包括基于股票的薪酬费用。
该公司将“调整后的EBITDA”作为对其业绩的补充衡量标准。公司调整后的EBITDA是通过调整GAAP中利息支出、其他收入(支出)、净额、所得税拨备(福利)、被投资人的股本收入(亏损)、基于股票的薪酬以及折旧和摊销费用来确定的。该公司还包括截至2019年6月29日的三个月和六个月的法律事项或有亏损。该公司计算和公布调整后的EBITDA是因为管理层认为，就其整体资本结构和借入额外资金的能力而言，它对投资者和贷款人都很重要。此外，该公司公布调整后的EBITDA是因为它认为这一衡量标准通过剔除本公司认为不能反映其核心经营业绩的项目，有助于投资者在一致的基础上比较不同报告期的业绩。本公司调整后EBITDA的计算可能与同行业其他公司的计算结果一致，也可能不一致。投资者不应将调整后的EBITDA视为衡量收入的GAAP运营指标或衡量运营、投资和融资活动现金流的GAAP流动性指标的替代方案。此外，调整后的EBITDA没有考虑可能影响现金流的某些资产和负债的变化。
该公司还公布自由现金流，作为对其业绩的非公认会计准则的补充衡量标准。自由现金流是通过调整由经营活动提供(用于)资本支出的GAAP净现金来确定的。公司在财务收益新闻稿中计算并传达自由现金流。

note_en:
Revenue grows 26 percent year-over-year driven by Ryzen™ and EPYC™ processor sales SANTA CLARA, Calif.,  July 28, 2020 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --  AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) today announced revenue for the second quarter of 2020 of  $1.93 billion, operating income of  $173 million, net income of  $157 million and diluted earnings per share of  $0.13. On a non-GAAP* basis, operating income was  $233 million, net income was  $216 million and diluted earnings per share was  $0.18.
     “We delivered strong second quarter results, led by record notebook and server processor sales as Ryzen and EPYC revenue more than doubled from a year ago,” said Dr.  Lisa Su, AMD president and CEO. “Despite some macroeconomic uncertainty, we are raising our full-year revenue outlook as we enter our next phase of growth driven by the acceleration of our business in multiple markets.”
  Revenue was  $1.93 billion, up 26 percent year-over-year primarily driven by higher Computing and Graphics segment revenue. Revenue was up 8 percent quarter-over-quarter primarily driven by higher Enterprise, Embedded and Semi-Custom segment revenue.
  Gross margin was 44 percent, up 3 percentage points year-over-year and down 2 percentage points quarter-over-quarter. The year-over-year increase was primarily driven by Ryzen™ and EPYC™ processor sales. The quarter-over-quarter decrease was due to increased semi-custom product sales.
  Operating income was  $173 million compared to operating income of  $59 million a year ago and  $177 million in the prior quarter. Non-GAAP operating income was  $233 million compared to operating income of  $111 million a year ago and  $236 million in the prior quarter. Operating income improved year-over-year primarily driven by revenue growth and a greater percentage of Ryzen and EPYC processor sales.
  Net income was  $157 million compared to net income of  $35 million a year ago and  $162 million in the prior quarter. Non-GAAP net income was  $216 million compared to net income of  $92 million a year ago and  $222 million in the prior quarter.
  Diluted earnings per share was  $0.13 compared to diluted earnings per share of  $0.03 a year ago and  $0.14 in the prior quarter. Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share was  $0.18 compared to diluted earnings per share of  $0.08 a year ago and  $0.18 in the prior quarter.
  Computing and Graphics segment revenue was  $1.37 billion, up 45 percent year-over-year and down 5 percent quarter-over-quarter. Revenue was higher year-over-year driven by strong Ryzen processor sales. The quarter-over-quarter decline was due to lower graphics processor sales.  Client processor average selling price (ASP) was up year-over-year driven by Ryzen processor sales. Client processor ASP was down quarter-over-quarter due to a higher percentage of Ryzen mobile processor sales.
    Operating income was  $200 million compared to  $22 million a year ago and  $262 million in the prior quarter. The year-over-year increase was driven by higher revenue. The quarter-over-quarter decline was due to higher operating expenses and lower revenue.
  Enterprise, Embedded and Semi-Custom segment revenue was  $565 million, down 4 percent year-over-year and up 62 percent quarter-over-quarter. Revenue was lower year-over-year due to lower semi-custom product sales largely offset by higher EPYC processor sales. The quarter-over-quarter increase was driven by higher EPYC processor and semi-custom product sales.   Operating income was  $33 million compared to  $89 million a year ago and an operating loss of  $26 million in the prior quarter. The year-over-year decline was due to higher operating expenses and lower revenue. The quarter-over-quarter increase was driven by higher revenue.
  All Other operating loss was  $60 million compared to operating losses of  $52 million a year ago and  $59 million in the prior quarter.
  Data center adoption of 2 nd Gen AMD EPYC processors continued to accelerate with unique cloud, enterprise and high-performance computing (HPC) wins.  Google  announced new Confidential Virtual Machines for  Google Compute Engine powered exclusively by AMD EPYC processors featuring AMD Secure Encrypted Virtualization. These Confidential VMs provide customers with data security and high-performance cloud computing enabled by advanced security features on AMD EPYC processors.
  Amazon Web Services (AWS)  added its sixth AMD EPYC processor-powered cloud instance family, the new Amazon EC2 C5a instances for customers running compute intensive workloads.
  Oracle and AMD  announced that AMD EPYC processors are bringing class-leading memory bandwidth and core count to the new Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Compute E3 platform for general purpose and high bandwidth workloads.
  Dell Technologies, HPE, IBM Cloud, Nutanix, Supermicro, VMware and others announced offerings powered by the new  AMD EPYC 7Fx2 processor family that bring high-frequency processing power and per-core performance leadership to enterprise workloads.
  Dell Technologies  announced its first hyperconverged infrastructure system, the VxRail E Series, based on AMD EPYC processors.
  The new Nvidia DGX A100 system  harnesses the performance, core count and PCIe® 4 support of AMD EPYC processors to accelerate diverse AI workloads such as data analytics, training and inference.
  Commercial momentum for AMD client processors is growing, driven by Ryzen PRO performance, security features and seamless management for the most demanding business environments.  Lenovo and AMD  announced the Lenovo ThinkStation P620 powered by AMD Ryzen Threadripper™ PRO processors, including a 64-core professional workstation CPU. The new AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO processor family features leadership performance, unrivalled bandwidth and  AMD PRO technologies which include enterprise-grade security and manageability features.
  AMD  announced the AMD Ryzen PRO 4000 Series mobile processors, bringing ultimate performance, work anywhere flexibility, security features and seamless manageability for modern IT infrastructures. Enterprise notebooks powered by the AMD Ryzen PRO 4000 Series are available now from  HP and  Lenovo.
  AMD  expanded its desktop processor portfolio with the 3 rd Gen AMD Ryzen 3000XT series desktop processors, featuring higher boost frequencies that deliver elite performance in gaming and content creation compared to prior generation.
  Apple  announced the latest 16-inch MacBook Pro featuring the AMD Radeon™ Pro 5600M mobile GPU, bringing desktop-class graphics performance to users on the go.
  AMD  announced the AMD Radeon™ Pro VII workstation graphics card with AMD Infinity Fabric™ Link technology, delivering exceptional graphics and computing performance for broadcast and engineering professionals.
  AMD  announced it is contributing cloud-based supercomputing systems powered by AMD EPYC processors and AMD Radeon Instinct accelerators as part of the  AMD HPC Fund for COVID-19 research. The systems will be delivered to  New York University (NYU),  Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) and  Rice University in partnership with Penguin Computing to provide remote supercomputing capabilities for selected researchers around the world.
  AMD’s outlook statements are based on current expectations and contemplate the current COVID-19 environment, global economic backdrop and customer demand signals. The following statements are forward-looking, and actual results could differ materially depending on market conditions, COVID-19 factors and the factors set forth under “Cautionary Statement” below.

据报道，墨西哥头号通缉犯之一El Mencho在西部的哈利斯科州建立了自己的私人医院。
这位卡特尔头目的真名是内梅西奥·奥塞格拉·塞万提斯(Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes)，近几个月来，他已成为该地区的头号恶棍，并在6月份因涉嫌在黎明前厚颜无耻地试图谋杀墨西哥城警察局长而登上新闻头条。
本月早些时候，社交媒体上疯传的一段视频据称显示了数十名全副武装的门乔步兵，这增加了这一恶名-尽管墨西哥政府质疑这段视频的真实性。
本周，关于墨西哥哈利斯科新一代卡特尔头目的新指控浮出水面，声称他为一家医院的建设提供了资金。
“环球报”(El Universal)援引情报报告称，这位54岁的毒枭在哈利斯科首府瓜达拉哈拉西南约250公里的村庄埃尔阿尔西瓦特尔(El Alcíhuatl)建了诊所。
拉美黑帮经常将医疗专业人员留在工资单上，以便在安全部队开枪的情况下避开官方医院。
但据报道，埃尔·门乔之所以决定建立自己的医院，是因为他不愿冒险走出他的农村藏身之处--据说El Alcíhuatl就是其中之一--寻求肾脏疾病的治疗。该报说，毒枭的员工和当地人也使用了这家诊所。
El Universal称村庄所在的直辖市Villa Purificación是哈利斯科卡特尔的主要据点之一。就是在这里，一架墨西哥军用直升机在2015年试图逮捕其领导人时被火箭推进手榴弹击落，但以失败告终。
从那时起，这个以西班牙语首字母CJNG闻名的组织变得越来越强大。2018年，美国当局宣布将其列为“头等大事”，宣布悬赏1000万美元寻找有助于逮捕门乔的信息。
投诉警察课的儿子在今年2月被引渡到美国面临贩毒指控，几天后他的女儿在华盛顿被捕。但是，已经取代现已入狱的“El Chapo”成为墨西哥最引人注目的黑帮头目的El Mencho仍然是自由的。
研究墨西哥卡特尔的有组织犯罪专家克里斯·达尔比(Chris Dalby)表示，当局目前认为门乔是“头号公敌”。
在他的指挥下，该卡特尔已发展成为墨西哥最强大的犯罪组织-向美国走私大量可卡因、大麻和合成毒品，如S-芬太尼，以及贩运武器、人口和燃料。
“哈利斯科卡特尔是现在流行的组织，”追踪拉丁美洲有组织犯罪的“洞察力犯罪”(Insight Critical)的执行主编达尔比(Dalby)说。
达尔比说，卡特尔利用新冠肺炎疫情，在墨西哥的萨卡特卡斯州、韦拉克鲁斯州和瓜纳华托州“积极扩张”。然而，人们对门乔本人的了解相对较少。
“他非常神秘。他没有表现出自己的…。他不是像El Chapo那样的普通人，El Chapo培养了个人崇拜，人们认为他与人打成一片，有点像巴勃罗·埃斯科瓦尔。他是毒枭进化的一部分，他们想把自己藏在阴影里。“。
该组织日益增长的形象意味着总统安德烈斯·曼努埃尔·洛佩斯·奥布拉多(Andrés Manuel López Obrador)面临越来越大的抓捕毒枭的压力。
达尔比说：“如果墨西哥军队齐心协力，我相信他们能找到门乔，就能消灭他。”
“(但)这不会有什么不同。这可能会削弱哈利斯科卡特尔，使其四分五裂。但这并不会让墨西哥变得更安全--因为会有其他人来接管。“
note_en:
One of Mexico’s most wanted drug lords, El Mencho, is  reported to have built his own private hospital in the western state of Jalisco.
  The cartel boss – whose real name is  Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes – has become the region’s pre-eminent villain in recent months and grabbed headlines in June after his assassins allegedly launched  a brazen pre-dawn attempt to murder Mexico City’s police chief.
 Earlier this month  a viral social media video purportedly showing scores of El Mencho’s heavily armed foot soldiers added to that notoriety – although  Mexico’s government has questioned the veracity of the footage.
 This week, fresh claims about the head of Mexico’s Jalisco New Generation cartel emerged, alleging he had bankrolled the construction of a hospital.
 Citing intelligence reports, the El Universal newspaper said the 54-year-old drug lord had built the clinic in El Alcíhuatl , a village about 250km south-west of Jalisco’s capital, Guadalajara.
 Latin American gangsters often  keep medical professionals on the payroll in order to avoid official hospitals in the event of being shot by security forces.
 But El Mencho’s decision to build his own hospital reportedly stems from his reluctance to venture out of his network of rural hideouts – of which El Alcíhuatl is supposedly one – to seek treatment for kidney disease. The newspaper said the drug lord’s employees and locals also used the clinic.
 El Universal called Villa Purificación, the municipality where the village is located, one of the Jalisco cartel’s key strongholds. It was here that a Mexican army helicopter was shot down with a rocket-propelled grenade  during a failed 2015 attempt to capture its leader.
 Since then the group – best known by its Spanish initials CJNG – has gone from strength to strength. In 2018 US authorities declared it a “top priority”, announcing  a $10m reward for information leading to El Mencho’s arrest.
 The capo’s son  was extradited to the US to face drug trafficking charges in February this year, and  his daughter arrested in Washington just days later. But El Mencho, who has replaced  the now jailed “El Chapo” as Mexico’s highest-profile gangster, remains free.
 Chris Dalby, an organized crime expert  who studies Mexico’s cartel, said authorities currently considered El Mencho “public enemy No 1”.
 Under his command, the cartel had evolved into Mexico’s most powerful criminal organization – smuggling huge quantities of cocaine, marijuana and synthetic drugs such a s fentanyl into the US, as well as trafficking arms, people and fuel.
 “The Jalisco cartel is the group that’s en vogue now,” said Dalby, the managing editor of InSight Crime, which tracks Latin American organized crime.
 Dalby said that the cartel had taken advantage of the Covid-19 pandemic to “aggressively expand” across Mexican states such as Zacatecas, Veracruz and Guanajuato. Yet relatively little was known about El Mencho himself.
 “He’s very secretive. He does not show himself … He’s not a man of the people like El Chapo, who cultivated that cult of personality, who was seen engaging with people, a little bit like Pablo Escobar. He’s part of an evolution of drug kingpins who want to keep themselves in the shadows.”
  The group’s growing profile meant President  Andrés Manuel López Obrador faced mounting pressure to catch the drug lord.
 “If the Mexican army made a concerted effort, I’m sure they could find El Mencho and they could rub him out,” Dalby said.
 “[But] it would make no difference. It would perhaps weaken the Jalisco cartel and fragment them. But it would not make  Mexico any safer – because someone else would just take over.”
title: 据报道，墨西哥顶级毒枭El Mencho建立了自己的私立医院
title_en: Top Mexican drug kingpin El Mencho reportedly builds own private hospital

这款应用程序与其他家长控制技术展开竞争，包括iOS和Android内置的技术-后者也可以作为独立的应用程序使用，名为Family Link。像它的竞争对手一样，微软家庭安全设置将最适合那些已经购买了公司自己的产品和服务生态系统的人。以微软为例，这包括Windows10个人电脑和Xbox设备。
与许多屏幕时间应用程序一样，家庭安全设置也会显示孩子们如何使用屏幕时间的活动日志。它可以跟踪花在包括Windows电脑、手机和Xbox在内的设备上的小时数，以及跨网站和应用程序的小时数。它还可以显示孩子们在网上搜索的术语。
每周一次的报告通过电子邮件发送给父母和孩子，希望能鼓励围绕健康使用屏幕时间的讨论。在大流行之前，这已经是一个复杂的话题。但现在，随着孩子们在家上学，用几个小时的游戏来打发暑假时间，而父母们仍然试图在没有孩子照看的情况下工作，情况变得更加复杂。
起初，父母可能只是完全放弃了在屏幕上的时间，感激任何给他们带来宁静时刻的东西。但是，随着呆在家里成为一种新的常态，许多家庭现在都在重新考虑看屏幕的时间多少才是健康的，多少时间才是太多。
有了这款新的应用程序，家长可以设置适用于包括Xbox在内的所有设备的屏幕时间限制。这些限制可以被狭隘地配置为允许访问促进在线学习的教育应用程序，同时限制其他类型的屏幕时间-例如游戏。当孩子们没有时间时，他们可以要求更多，父母可以选择是否给予。
与此同时，新应用程序的网页过滤功能利用了微软较新的浏览器Microsoft Edge，涵盖了Windows、Xbox和Android。这款应用程序将允许家长设置搜索过滤器并屏蔽成熟的内容。如果孩子试图从Microsoft应用商店下载成熟的游戏或应用程序，其他内容控件也会通知家长。
父母还可以通过批准孩子的请求来控制购买，这样以后就不会有令人惊讶的账单了。
此外，这款应用的内置位置共享意味着，家庭可以跳过下载Life360等额外的家庭定位器应用，以访问基本的位置跟踪功能-比如那些在地图上显示家庭成员的应用，并让你保存最喜欢的位置，比如“家”(Home)。
自预览期以来，微软已经扩展了这款应用的功能，包括一些新功能，包括允许你屏蔽和解除屏蔽特定应用的功能，位置集群功能，以及一系列扩展的选项，以获得更多的屏幕时间(例如15或30分钟，1、2或3小时等)。辅助功能选项也进行了更新和改进，包括为视力较低的用户改进了视觉对比度，并为屏幕阅读器增加了上下文。
然而，你会注意到，家庭安全的一些体验并没有完全延伸到iOS和Android，比如购买控制和网页过滤。在iOS上，这款应用甚至无法跟踪屏幕时间的使用情况，因为苹果没有提供这方面的API，即使在推出了自己的屏幕时间服务并关闭了竞争对手的应用程序之后也是如此。
这是因为其他平台锁定了自己的操作系统和生态系统，以鼓励客户只购买和使用他们的设备。不幸的是，这意味着拥有来自不同供应商的设备的家庭-比如也在Xbox上玩游戏的iPhone用户，或者电脑是Mac的Android用户-没有简单的工具可以让他们从一个地方管理一切。
微软表示，在家庭安全设置推出后，它将很快推出两个新功能。这些功能包括位置警报和驾驶安全(例如针对青少年司机)，并且将成为付费Microsoft 365系列订阅的一部分。
新的家庭安全设置应用程序现在正在iOS和Android上推出，免费下载。由于这款应用的阶段性推出，你可能无法立即访问它，但应该在本周某个时候访问。
note_en:
The app competes with other parental control technologies, including those built into iOS and Android — the latter of which is also available as a standalone app, called  Family Link. Like its competitors, Microsoft Family Safety will work best for those who have already bought into the company’s own ecosystem of products and services. In Microsoft’s case, that includes Windows 10 PCs and Xbox devices, for example.
 Also like many screen time apps, Family Safety displays an activity log of how screen time is being used by kids. It can track the hours spent on devices, including Windows computers, phones, and Xbox, as well as across websites and apps. It can also show the terms kids are searching for online.
   A weekly report is emailed to parents and kids, with the hopes of encouraging discussions around healthy use of screen time. This was already a complicated subject before the pandemic. But now, with kids attending school at home and filling summer downtime with hours in games while parents still try to work without childcare, it’s grown to be even more complicated.
 Initially, parents may have just given up on screen time altogether, grateful for anything that allowed them that gave them moments of peace. But with staying at home becoming a new normal, many families are now reconsidering what amount of screen time is healthy and how much is too much.
 With the new app, parents can set screen time limits that apply across devices — including Xbox. These limits can be narrowly configured to allow for access to educational apps that facilitate online learning, while limiting other types of screen time — like gaming, for instance. When kids run out of time, they can ask for more and parents can choose whether or not to grant it.
 Meanwhile, the web filtering aspects of the new app take advantage of Microsoft’s newer browser, Microsoft Edge across Windows, Xbox, and Android. The app will allow parents to set search filters and block mature content. Other content controls will notify parents if the child tries to download a mature game or app from the Microsoft Store, as well.
   Parents can also control purchases by granting approval to kids’ requests, so there won’t be surprise bills later.
 Plus, the app’s built-in location sharing means families can skip downloading additional family locator apps, like Life360, for access to basic location tracking features — like those that show family members on a map and lets you save favorite locations, like “Home.”
  Since its preview period, Microsoft has expanded the app’s capabilities to include a handful of new features, including one that lets you block and unblock specific apps, a location clustering feature, and an expanded set of options for granting more screen time (e.g 15 or 30 mins., 1, 2, or 3 hours, etc.). Accessibility options were also updated and improved, including improved visual contrast for low vision users and additional context for screen readers.
 You’ll note, however, that some of Family Safety’s experience don’t fully extend to iOS and Android, like purchase controls and web filtering. On iOS, the app can’t even track screen time usage as Apple makes no API available for this, even after launching its own screen time service and  shutting down competing apps.
 That’s due to how other platforms have their own operating systems and ecosystems locked down to encourage customers to only buy and use their devices. Unfortunately, that means families that have devices from a variety of vendors — like iPhone users who also game on Xbox, or Android users whose computer is a Mac, for instance — don’t have simple tools that let them manage everything from one place.
 Microsoft says it will soon roll out two new features to Family Safety following its launch. These include location alerts and drive safety (e.g. aimed teen drivers),, and will be a part of a paid Microsoft 365 Family Subscription.
 The new Family Safety app is rolling out now for  iOS and  Android as a free download. You may not be able to immediately access the app due to its phased rollout, but should sometime this week.
title: 微软新的家庭安全设置应用程序提供跨手机、PC和Xbox的家长控制
title_en: Microsoft’s new Family Safety app offers parental controls across phones, PCs and Xbox

微软上周宣称，它在20财年的商用云收入已经超过500亿美元。与往常一样，在披露财报时，官员们没有公开透露包括Azure在内的各种服务对这一总收入的贡献。但官员们确实私下向一些员工和合作伙伴提供了更多的突破。据我要求不具名的联系人透露，微软首席财务官艾米·胡德(Amy Hood)在微软内部准备会议的一次会议上讨论了几个额外的数据点，其中包括：
我的联系人胡德告诉准备好的与会者，微软365--其Windows/Office 365/Enterprise Mobility+Security捆绑包--总共为微软200亿美元的财年贡献了1430亿美元的账单收入。这比微软在19年从微软365获得的132亿美元增长了50%以上。
官员们在财报电话会议上表示，微软价格最高、功能最丰富的SKU Microsoft365E5/Office365E5的需求在20财年翻了一番。但胡德私下分享了更多：在20财年，E5贡献了75亿美元，高于19财年的42亿美元。她告诉员工和合作伙伴，Office 365 E5只占Office 365空缺席位的5%。
Dynamic 365的收入(我相信这意味着微软Dynamics 365应用程序和服务的云端和内部版本)在20财年为22亿美元，高于19财年的16亿美元。
Azure：胡德表示，微软第四季度的目标是实现60%的增长(一些微软没有披露的基数，甚至是私下向员工和合作伙伴披露的基数)；这一目标是59%。交易规模继续扩大，越来越多的Azure交易落入1000万美元以上的类别。
微软在2015年创建了其商业云度量。当时，官员们表示，他们预计到2018年，该公司的商业云年运行率将达到200亿美元。微软在2017年10月实际上超过了商业云每年200亿美元的运行率。
微软商业云的主要服务是Azure；Office 365商业服务(Exchange Online、SharePoint Online、Skype for Business Online、Microsoft Teams)；Dynamics 365云服务；LinkedIn商业服务及其企业移动+安全套件(EMS)。内部部署产品不包括在总数中。我问过微软，目前微软365的所有收入是否都被算作商业云的一部分。到目前为止还没有回音。Microsoft 365 E5的年度承诺费用为每个用户每月57美元。Microsoft 365 E3的价格为每个用户每月32美元，而Microsoft 365 F3(以前称为F1，Firstline Worker SKU)的价格为每个用户每月10美元。E5 SKU包括会议和语音、分析、身份和管理、威胁防护以及其他两个主要Microsoft 365 SKU没有的功能。微软预计将在今年秋天推出其Project Cortex知识管理服务，这是一项仅对E5用户可用的高级功能。微软曾希望在今年夏初让Cortex全面上市，但发布被推迟了。
note_en:
Microsoft trumpeted last week that it had  surpassed $50 billion in "commercial cloud" revenues in FY'20. As usual, officials didn't share publicly during the disclosure of its fiscal results how much its various services, including Azure, contributed to that total. But officials did provide a bit more of a breakout privately to some employees and partners.    Among a few of the additional data points Microsoft Chief Financial Officer Amy Hood discussed during a session at Microsoft's internal Ready conference, according to my contacts, who asked not to be named:
 Microsoft 365 -- its Windows/Office 365/Enterprise Mobility + Security bundles -- contributed $20 billion in billed revenues to Microsoft's FY'20 $143 billion total, Hood told Ready attendees, my contact said. That was up more than 50% over the $13.2 billion that Microsoft garnered from Microsoft 365 in FY'19.
 Demand for Microsoft 365 E5/Office 365 E5, Microsoft's highest priced and most feature-rich SKU,  doubled in FY'20, officials said during the earnings call. But Hood privately shared more: In FY'20, E5 contributed $7.5 billion, up from $4.2 billion in FY'19. She told employees and partners that Office 365 E5 represents only 5% of the Office 365 seats available.
 Dynamics 365 revenues (which I believe means both cloud and on-prem versions of Microsoft's Dynamics 365 apps and services) were $2.2 billion in FY'20, up from $1.6 billion in FY'19.
 Azure: Hood said Microsoft's goal for Q4 was to grow 60% (of some base which Microsoft has not disclosed, even privately to employees and partners);  it came in at 59%. Deal sizes continue to get bigger, with a growing number of Azure deals falling into the $10 million-plus category.
 Microsoft created its commercial cloud metric in 2015. At that time, officials said they expected the company to  hit a $20 billion annual run rate for commercial cloud by 2018. Microsoft actually surpassed the $20 billion annual run rate for commercial cloud in October 2017.
 The  primary services that are part of Microsoft's commercial cloud are Azure; Office 365 business services (Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Skype for Business Online, Microsoft Teams); Dynamics 365 cloud services; "commercial" LinkedIn services and its Enterprise Mobility + Security Suite (EMS). On-premises products are not included in the total.    I've asked Microsoft if all Microsoft 365 revenues currently are counted as part of the commercial cloud. No word back so far.     Microsoft 365 E5 costs $57 per user per month for an annual commitment. Microsoft 365 E3 goes for $32 per user per month, and Microsoft 365 F3 (formerly known as F1, the firstline worker SKU) goes for $10 per user per month. The E5 SKU includes meetings and voice, analytics, identity and management, threat protection, and other features not in the other two main Microsoft 365 SKUs.    Microsoft is expected to make its  Project Cortex knowledge management service  a premium feature available only to E5 subscribers once the service launches this fall. Microsoft was hoping to make Cortex generally available early this summer, but the launch is delayed.
title: 来源：微软365在20财年贡献了200亿美元的账单收入，同比增长50%；其最昂贵的SKU E5增加了75亿美元的收入，占所有席位的5%
title_en: Sources: Microsoft 365 contributed $20B in billed revenues in FY'20, up 50%+ YoY; its priciest SKU, E5, added $7.5B in revenue and makes up 5% of all seats

微软本周将为iOS和Android推出一款新的家庭安全设置应用程序。近几个月来，这家软件巨头一直在测试这款应用，它的设计目的是让家庭管理屏幕和应用程序的使用。家长可以使用家庭安全设置应用程序来接收关于应用程序使用情况和总体屏幕的报告，甚至可以设置应用程序的时间限制，让孩子可以请求延期。
Microsoft家庭安全设置还可以在Windows、Xbox和Android之间同步，因此游戏和应用程序的时间限制将在多个设备上同步。这防止了孩子们交换设备来获得一些额外的时间来玩“我的世界”或“堡垒之夜”。特定的应用程序也可以被完全屏蔽。
微软还添加了网页和搜索过滤器，以限制浏览儿童友好的网站。这些过滤器适用于Windows、Xbox和Android上的Microsoft Edge浏览器。这些屏幕和内容过滤器大多仅限于移动端的Android，但微软表示正在努力将其引入iPhone。甚至还可以通过购买请求电子邮件锁定账户上的突击消费，帮助防止数千V-bucks的信用卡账单。
家庭安全也可用于家庭成员之间的位置共享。微软在测试阶段增加了一个新的位置集群功能，它可以让人们看到多个家庭成员在同一个位置。微软还计划在家人到达或离开某个地点时添加通知，但这将是Microsoft365订户的高级功能。
微软365用户还将推出一项新的驱动器安全功能。它旨在帮助人们建立更好的驾驶习惯，并提供“关于驾驶行为的见解”。微软没有说它将在什么时候到达，但这一驱动器安全功能将在美国、英国、澳大利亚和加拿大推出。
如果你有兴趣尝试微软家庭安全设置应用程序，它现在可以在Google Play Store上买到，本周晚些时候将在苹果的App Store上推出。
更新，美国东部时间7月28日12点35分：微软计划本周晚些时候在iOS上发布这款应用。
note_en:
Microsoft is launching a new Family Safety app for iOS and Android this week. The software giant has been  testing the app in recent months, and it’s designed to allow families to manage screentime and app usage. Parents can use the Family Safety app to receive reports on app usage and overall screentime and even set app time limits with the ability for children to request extensions.
 Microsoft Family Safety also syncs across Windows, Xbox, and Android, so time limits for games and apps will be synced across multiple devices. This prevents kids from swapping devices to get some extra hours to play  Minecraft or  Fortnite. Specific apps can also be fully blocked.
   Microsoft has also added web and search filters to limit browsing to kid-friendly sites. These filters work across Microsoft’s Edge browser on Windows, Xbox, and Android. Most of these screentime and content filters are limited to Android on the mobile side, but Microsoft says it’s working on bringing them to iPhones, too. There’s even the ability to lock down surprise spending on accounts with purchase request emails, helping prevent credit card bills for thousands of V-bucks.
 Family Safety can also be used for location sharing between family members. Microsoft has added a new location-clustering feature during the beta phase, which lets people see when multiple family members are at the same location. Microsoft is also planning to add notifications for when family members arrive or leave a location, but this will be a premium feature for Microsoft 365 subscribers.
   A new drive safety feature is also on the way for Microsoft 365 subscribers. It’s designed to help people build better driving habits and provide “insights on driving behavior.” Microsoft isn’t saying exactly when it will arrive, but this drive safety feature will launch in the US, UK, Australia, and Canada.
 If you’re interested in trying out the Microsoft Family Safety app, it’s now available on the  Google Play Store and will be available Apple’s App Store later this week.
 Update, July 28th 12:35ET:  Microsoft is planning to launch the app on iOS later this week.
title: 微软为iOS和Android推出新的家庭安全设置应用程序，用户可以管理屏幕时间和应用程序使用情况
title_en: Microsoft launches a new Family Safety app for iOS and Android that lets users manage screen time and app usage
